description: 15-turn conversation. User corrects a preference at turn 8. Tests misinterpretation conflict resolution and supersession chain.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: I prefer my reports in bullet points.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Noted — I will use bullet points for reports.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Can you summarise last week's sprint?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Here is a bullet-point summary of last week's sprint: ...

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: The deadline for the Q3 report is June 15th.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Understood. Q3 report deadline is June 15th.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Can you draft the intro for the Q3 report?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Actually, when I said bullet points, I meant technically precise prose, not short bullets. I need depth.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Understood — I misread that. You want technically precise prose with depth, not short bullets. I will update my understanding.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Yes, exactly. Can you redo the sprint summary with that in mind?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Here is the sprint summary as technically precise prose: ...

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Much better. Also the Q3 deadline moved to June 20th.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Updated — Q3 report deadline is now June 20th.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: What format do you use for my reports?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Technically precise prose with depth — you clarified this at turn 8 after I had initially understood it as bullet points.
